sourceMovie.title: Garfield
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Garfield works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Garfield clicked for you, Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Garfield?
faq.answer: Try Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted, Lady and the Tramp, The Secret Life of Pets 2—each shares DNA with Garfield while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Garfield?
faq.answer: Garfield (2004) is best described as Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Garfield part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Garfield (Live-Action/CGI) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
